MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS
Off Calendar Dec-12-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS.

Wells Fargo Bank N.A.'s motion for judgment on the pleadings The court considers not only facts pleaded but also judicial admissions that cannot be controverted. (Pang v. Beverly Hosp., Inc. (2000) 79 Cal.App.4th 986, 989.) Defendant Christian Bonifacio is subject to a deemed-admitted order and he may not controvert matters that are deemed admitted. (Order Granting Motion to Deem Requests for Admission, Oct. 8, 2025.) These admissions establish Wells Fargo's entitlement to judgment on its single-count breach of contract cause of action; they establish contract, breach, performance by plaintiff, and damages. (RJN, Ex. B.) The court grants the motion. Wells Fargo shall submit a separate judgment after complying with Rule of Court 3.1312.

proposed order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Deem Requests For Admissions Admitted And Of Nonappearance
Off Calendar Sep-05-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'s Motion To Deem Requests For Admissions Admitted And Of Nonappearance.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Wells Fargo Bank, N.A.'s motion to deem admitted the truth of any matters specified in its Requests for Admission, Set One, is granted. Defendant Christian D. Bonifacio failed to timely respond to this discovery and has thereby waived objections. (Code Civ. Proc., section 2033.280, subd. (a).) The truth of any matters specified in the RFAs, Set Two, is deemed admitted by Bonifacio. (Id. section 2033.280, subds. (b), (c).)
Wells Fargo does not seek sanctions, but Code of Civil Procedure, section 2033.280, subdivision (c) further provides: "It is mandatory that the court impose a monetary sanction under Chapter 7 (commencing with Section 2023.010) on the party or attorney, or both, whose failure to serve a timely response to requests for admission necessitated this motion." The court awards sanctions of $50 payable by Bonifacio to Wells Fargo within 30 days of notice of entry of this order.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
